summary: A single fourslash test failed because the 'Add return type' code fix produced an oversimplified generic type annotation. The fix emitted 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' but the test expected a fully-parameterized form beginning 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string…'. All other 106,366 tests passed.
root_cause: The code fix for missing type annotations on exports incorrectly simplifies generic return types, emitting 'Foo<string>' instead of the expected multi-parameter form 'Foo<string, string[…]', causing an AssertionError in the fourslash test harness.
relevant_tests: fourslash tests > tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts > fourslash test codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts runs correctly

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: This is the direct assertion mismatch: the actual code fix title is missing one or more type parameters compared to the expected value.
Confirms exactly one test failed, and the context identifies it as the fourslash test above.
The failure originates in the verifyCodeFix method, confirming this is a code-fix output mismatch, not a compilation or infrastructure error.
The test name itself signals the scenario being tested: oversimplification of generics in the missing-type-annotation code fix, which matches the observed failure (type parameters being dropped).

suggested_fix: Update the code fix logic responsible for inferring return type annotations on exported declarations to preserve all type arguments when constructing the generic type node (e.g., 'Foo<string>' should be 'Foo<string, string[]>' or similar), so the generated label matches the expectation in tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts.
